entries.mem0ai/mem0: The official Hermes Agent memory provider, offering a universal layer for user, session, and agent state. It supports hybrid retrieval via semantic, keyword, and entity matching across managed cloud or self-hosted deployments.
entries.supermemoryai/supermemory: An official Hermes Agent provider featuring a custom vector graph engine and context fencing for high-speed recall. It maintains persistent memory graphs and automated user profiling to blend static facts with dynamic activity.
entries.volcengine/OpenViking: An official open-source context database that uses tiered L0/L1/L2 loading to significantly reduce token consumption. It organizes memories and skills via a filesystem-based approach and provides visualized retrieval trajectories for debugging.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: An opinionated brain for Hermes Agents that automatically extracts typed entity relationships into a self-wiring knowledge graph. Its synthesis layer generates cited prose answers and performs explicit gap analysis.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: A learning-focused memory system that utilizes retain, recall, and reflect workflows for long-term knowledge management. It acts as an LLM wrapper to automate storage and retrieval across providers like OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: A stateful memory library that extracts reasoning-based conclusions from conversations rather than simple text matching. It tracks multi-peer perspectives to model what different entities know about one another.
entries.campfirein/byterover-cli: An official provider that treats memory as a git repository, enabling versioning, branching, and merging of context trees. It features a sub-100ms 5-tier retrieval system and integrates with the Model Context Protocol.
entries.greyhaven/autocontext: A recursive self-improving harness that distills successful agent strategies into reusable playbooks. It provides structured output via trace.jsonl logs and reports to help agents master complex tasks.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: A zero-dependency memory system utilizing a SQLite backend for sub-millisecond retrieval performance. It manages long-term knowledge through episodic compression and temporal triples.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: A lossless context management plugin that uses a hierarchical DAG for depth-aware compaction. It ensures no messages are lost by persisting raw payloads in SQLite with dedicated drill-down query tools.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: A self-hosted backend for the Honcho library that enables private, cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent. It supports local inference servers and OpenAI-compatible providers via a Docker-based setup.
entries.Signet-AI/signetai: A local-first identity and secrets layer that provides portable state across Hermes Agent and other MCP clients. It includes a repairable memory system for editing, deleting, or reclassifying agent context.
entries.plur-ai/plur: A shared memory layer for multi-agent systems using an open YAML engram format to eliminate API costs. It employs ACT-R activation to strengthen relevant facts while decaying stale information over time.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: A local-first context engine that removes cloud dependencies for on-device agent memory. It combines BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs, integrating via MCP servers and Claude Code hooks.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: A context optimization tool that increases cache hits and reduces prefill latency through prefix reordering and deduplication. It provides native plugin support to accelerate inference for Hermes Agent and OpenClaw.
entries.yantrikos/yantrikdb-hermes-plugin: An embedded, self-maintaining plugin that provides explainable recall through 'why_retrieved' tags. It features contradiction tracking and canonicalization of duplicates without requiring a separate server.
entries.yepyhun/Brainstack: A memory kernel stack that consolidates temporal continuity, graph truth, and corpus retrieval. It utilizes a multi-layered storage approach with SQLite, Kuzu, and Chroma to reduce prompt noise.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: A reflective memory system combining BM25 full-text search with ranked graph traversal. It automatically indexes local directories, URLs, and multimedia files using edge tags to create navigable relationships.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: An anticipatory memory system that prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs. It uses a local-first architecture with SQLite-vec and GGUF models via an MCP-native server.
entries.alias8818/hermes-tool-slimmer: A utility that reduces prompt token overhead by using local BM25 ranking to select only the most relevant tool schemas. It includes a dashboard to track estimated token savings.
entries.Ladybug-Memory/hermes-memory-plugin: A local-only plugin using an embedded .lbdb graph database with importance-weighted recall. It surfaces high-priority memories first and supports optional GLiNER2 entity extraction.
entries.MukundaKatta/hermes-agentmemory: A pull-model episodic memory plugin that ensures real deletion of events to prevent outdated summaries from affecting recall. It provides a trace.jsonl audit log for every memory operation.
entries.futurebrowser/hermes-exabase-plugin: A plugin that integrates Exabase M-1 to provide self-organizing long-term memory. It offers tools for manual storage and configurable query expansion to improve retrieval accuracy.

entries.nexu-io/open-design: A local-first open-source design and prototyping system that generates prototypes, decks, and HyperFrames motion graphics. It integrates with 21 local CLIs via an MCP server and provides 150 design systems and 261 plugins for Hermes Agent.
entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: A structured library of 754 cybersecurity skills mapped to major frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF 2.0, and NIST AI RMF. It is compatible with the agentskills.io standard to enable sub-second skill discovery across 26 security domains.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: Enables the generation of draw.io diagrams from natural language, supporting presets for ERD, UML, and Architecture. It provides automated structure diagrams for multiple languages and access to over 10,000 official shapes.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: An auditing tool that eliminates detectable AI writing patterns using a tiered word replacement table and 48 pattern categories. It offers Rewrite, Detect, and Edit-in-place modes and is compatible with Hermes and Claude Code.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: An agentic evolver that automates skill deduplication and quality improvement through background evolution loops. It unifies skill libraries across devices and teams with native integration for Hermes and OpenAI-compatible APIs.
entries.wondelai/skills: A cross-platform skills library providing modular frameworks for architecture, UX design, marketing, and product strategy. It supports installation via a marketplace or npx CLI for Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible agents.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: A meta-skill plugin that monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose reusable skills. It generates Python plugin scaffolds and SKILL.md documentation, manageable via dedicated slash commands.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: Provides type-safe agentskills.io support for Pydantic AI using progressive disclosure to reduce token usage. It supports both programmatic and filesystem-based Python skill definitions.
entries.ZeroPointRepo/youtube-skills: A suite of skills for extracting YouTube transcripts, captions, and timestamps without relying on yt-dlp. It supports video search, channel browsing, and playlist extraction for Hermes Agent and other AI agents.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: A community hub for browsing and one-click installation of skills, featuring a creator marketplace with crypto and Stripe payments. It ensures reliability through 65+ threat detection rules and an agent-to-agent feedback system.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Teaches Hermes Agent to write its own analytical prompts and custom thinking instructions for complex problems. It identifies structural conservation laws in codebases and generates reports on analysis blind spots.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: A harness for long-running agents featuring structured memory, gated lifecycles for tasks, and a plan-approve-execute workflow. It uses local-first plain-file storage for work and proof, integrating with Claude Code and Codex agents.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: A literate programming skill that weaves code and documentation into .lit.md files. It uses a tangler to extract runnable source files and automatically syncs edits back to the literate document.
entries.AkoliteZA/hermes-agent-idea-workflow: A pre-build workflow that transforms rough ideas into structured design documents and implementation handoffs. It offers Lite and Full modes and includes a spec review gate with PASS/FAIL evaluation models.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: A self-memory system that uses markdown-based fabric directories for cross-instance shared memory. It includes a pipeline for extracting training data with decision-quality tagging to fine-tune replacement models.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: Provides agentskills.io-compliant tools for interacting with Oracle networks and smart contracts. It supports CCIP cross-chain messaging, VRF v2.5 management, and Data Feeds integration.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Converts repositories, documentation, and developer discourse into agentskills.io-compliant skills. It supports version-pinned, immutable skills and links instructions directly to specific file lines or URLs.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: Official FLUX image generation skills providing standardized prompting guidelines for FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 models. It includes API integration patterns for webhooks and polling, supporting precise hex color specifications.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: A registry of reusable AI skills based on the AgentSkills specification, featuring an OpenAPI-driven Python client. It supports versioned uploads with recursive dependency resolution and integrated TON and Stripe payments.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: Enables autonomous execution of complex, multi-step tasks with real-time progress tracking. It includes checkpoints and built-in rollback procedures for safe recovery during long-running operations.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: A progressive MCP client that uses three-layer lazy loading to minimize token consumption. It features a socket-based daemon for persistent server management and supports hot-reloading of JSON configurations.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: A Hermes Agent skill for managing Nextcloud files, notes, calendars, and contacts via WebDAV and CalDAV. It ensures secure communication via HTTPS and includes a guided setup script for credential validation.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: A C# / .NET implementation of the agentskills.io specification with MCP integration. It utilizes SKILL.md files for progressive disclosure and integrates with Microsoft.Extensions.AI for tool loading.
entries.Sahil-SS9/hermes-multichannel-prompt-opt: A plugin that rewrites and scores prompts across various surfaces like Discord, Telegram, and CLI. It reduces token usage by 20–80% by removing noise and tailoring structure to specific model capabilities.
entries.willingning-coder/eagle-eye: An intelligent retrieval system using a 5-layer approach including FTS5, dense embeddings, and RRF fusion. It filters hundreds of skills down to the five most relevant to reduce token consumption.
entries.mag3nt-com/openclaw-skill: Automates payments for HTTP 402 responses using USDC via protocols like Pay Link and x402. It is compatible with Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, and the agentskills.io standard.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: A collection of Nix packages that simplifies the installation of Hermes and other AI coding agents. It ensures current software versions through automated daily updates across multiple provider toolsets.
entries.marshallrichards/ClawPhone: Enables the execution of agent CLIs on Android via Termux, providing hardware access through Termux:API and Termux:GUI. It includes specific configuration guides for running OpenClaw agents on ARM64 devices.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows desktop distribution featuring a GUI and 100 built-in tools. It supports local model execution via LM Studio and includes a dynamic tool maker for custom automation pipelines.
entries.AbuZar-Ansarii/Hermes-Agent-On-Android: Provides a one-line installation script to deploy Hermes Agent within Termux environments. This setup supports over 200 models, including local execution via Ollama for enhanced privacy.
entries.ultraworkers/hermes-agent-helm-chart: A community Helm chart for Kubernetes deployments that implements cloud-native state-safety guardrails. It ensures single-writer safety for persistent storage and offers a configurable gateway mode.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: A security-focused deployment that utilizes kernel-level enforcement to restrict network, filesystem, and process limits. It allows for dynamic security policies ranging from strict to permissive access.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: A streamlined Docker sandbox image that bundles the official Hermes installation with mini-swe-agent. It supports persistent state through mounted home directories and customizable build tags.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: A NixOS module and package that enables declarative management of agent configurations and systemd services. It integrates sops-nix for secure secrets handling and supports hybrid skill management.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: A production-ready headless deployment that configures Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service. It is designed for Ubuntu and Debian servers to execute recurring AI cron jobs autonomously.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: A Docker-first control plane that allows users to operate multiple Hermes Agent instances from a single web application. It features hierarchy-aware task delegation and enterprise MCP access for external integrations.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: A Portainer-managed Docker Compose stack that integrates a ttyd web terminal for remote access. The deployment is automated via GitHub Actions and managed as a Git Repository stack.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: A self-hosted orchestration layer that allows users to dispatch tasks and monitor spending across multi-agent workflows. It features a dependency-free SQLite stack and the Aegis review system to ensure high-quality task gates across CrewAI, LangGraph, and AutoGen.
entries.agent-of-empires/agent-of-empires: A management surface providing TUI and PWA dashboards to monitor various coding agents, including Hermes and Claude Code. It ensures secure execution through Docker, Podman, and Apple Container sandboxing while utilizing git worktrees for parallel agent operations.
entries.CryptoDmitry/hermes-agent-control-room: A control-room template designed to scale Hermes deployments from a single VPS agent to complex specialist teams. It implements a sidecar control plane for governance and utilizes task bus patterns for structured delegation.
entries.Agent-3-7/minions: A supervision hub that lets users create and review autonomous Hermes Agent work via a centralized screen. It features a Kanban board for status tracking and live streaming of agent reasoning for human-in-the-loop verification.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: A framework for building autonomous agent swarms with support for over 23 LLM providers, including Ollama and Hermes. It manages swarms through heartbeats and schedules, featuring durable memory and the ability to learn new skills from conversations.
entries.shannhk/hermes-agent-control-room: A scaling template that uses a level-based architecture to transition from single agents to automated teams. It includes a task bus for handoffs and bundled skills for VPS provisioning, security auditing, and backup management.
entries.linke-ai/hermes-agent-team: A local collaboration system that orchestrates Leader and Specialist agents using MCP and ACP protocols. It provides a real-time Web UI to monitor agent dialogues and manage the task lifecycle via Hermes Kanban.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: A specialized collective of 17 agents dedicated to research, planning, and implementation. The system enforces strict quality gates through mandatory reviewer and tester agents to secure infrastructure and refactoring pipelines.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: A meta-optimization harness that uses automated outer-loop searches to refine agent code. It orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmark evaluations to track performance frontiers through structured mutation and comparison.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: An agent-vs-agent arena where autonomous AI companies compete for GitHub stars. It orchestrates nine agents using Paperclip and Hermes Agent, visualizing the competition via a real-time scoreboard and task dashboard.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: An ACP-style delegation tool that routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code. It prevents parent context pollution through isolated workflows and enforces safety via configurable timeouts and output limits.

entries.colbymchenry/codegraph: A local pre-indexed code knowledge graph that reduces token usage and tool calls for Hermes Agent. By providing local semantic intelligence, it lowers average costs by 16% and tool calls by 58% without requiring Node.js.
entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: A Rust-powered CLI tool featuring a TUI and visualization dashboard to track token consumption and costs. It provides centralized monitoring across various AI coding agents, including Hermes and Gemini.
entries.liaohch3/claude-tap: A local trace viewer designed to inspect API traffic, system prompts, and tool schemas for Hermes Agent and other clients. It allows developers to compare adjacent requests to pinpoint specific changes in agent context.
entries.Salomondiei08/oh-my-hermes: An opinionated workflow layer that orchestrates 23 skills to automate the building, shipping, and monitoring of applications. It integrates with tools like Vercel and Sentry to handle everything from GitHub issue triage to security reviews.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: A collection of community-driven CLI skins and themes for customizing the Hermes terminal UI. Users can modify ASCII art, banner colors, and response labels using a flexible YAML configuration system.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: A port of the Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel. This integration eliminates the need for OAuth or external processes while adding MLOps tools for vLLM and RL training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: An AI-native browser that enables autonomous agent control via MCP integration. It features a human-in-the-loop interface with action approvals, rollbacks, and durable session management.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: A TAW Agent framework that enables the creation of scoped Hermes Agents with per-folder configurations. It allows users to seamlessly switch between default and project-specific memories, tools, and instructions.
entries.adityahimaone/hermes-agent-rtk-caveman: A preconfigured setup combining RTK and Caveman to achieve 90-99% token savings. It includes over 40 specialized skills for research, finance, and devops with automated installation for Linux and macOS.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: A rapid deployment stack that uses Docker to run Hermes Agent with LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant. It provides a zero-cost configuration featuring 29 community plugins for memory and quality validation.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: A deterministic static linter that scans agent configurations and system prompts for failure modes without using LLM calls. It utilizes seven structural detectors and supports CI/CD integration via JSON output.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: A migration utility that transfers personas, memories, and workspace files from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent. It maps complex model configurations to Hermes YAML format and handles the transfer of system-level credentials.

entries.farion1231/cc-switch: A cross-platform manager built with Tauri 2 that provides a unified interface for Hermes Agent, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and other agent CLIs. It simplifies the management of multiple AI tools across Windows, macOS, and Linux.
entries.iOfficeAI/AionUi: A local cowork application that integrates Hermes Agent and 13+ other CLI agents into a single GUI. It features a built-in engine for web search and MCP tool integration, with the ability to automatically generate editable Word, Excel, and Morph PPT documents.
entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: A lightweight web-based interface built with Python and vanilla JavaScript that maintains full feature parity with the Hermes CLI. It utilizes a three-panel layout for efficient workspace file browsing and inline previews.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: A desktop companion app designed for the installation and configuration of the Hermes Agent backend. It includes support for 16 messaging gateways, multiple AI providers, and integrated tools for persona editing and scheduled tasks.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A comprehensive web dashboard focused on session management and multi-platform configuration for 8 messaging services like Discord and Slack. It provides detailed usage analytics for token tracking, cost estimation, and model distribution.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: A native web workspace featuring a PTY terminal and Monaco editor for full file system access. It introduces Swarm Mode for role-based dispatch using tmux-backed workers alongside an integrated MCP catalog and memory browser.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A native macOS workspace that connects directly via SSH without the need for browser wrappers or daemons. It provides dedicated views for Kanban boards, cron jobs, and session data across multiple Hermes profiles.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: A self-hosted web dashboard featuring the ZOO Swarm Monitor with a kanban pipeline and live event feed. It offers a full MCP server control plane with log tailing and a browser-based terminal for system management.
entries.jazzyalex/agent-sessions: A native macOS session browser and 'agent cockpit' for monitoring live sessions and usage limits. It provides local-only indexing for searching AI agent histories without any remote data uploads or telemetry.
entries.nickvasilescu/hermes-desktop-os1: A macOS interface specifically designed for Hermes Agent instances running on Orgo cloud computers and SSH hosts. It features one-click automated installation on cloud VMs and integrated WebRTC voice mode via OpenAI Realtime.
entries.Eynzof/hermes-agent-cn-desktop: A Windows-first desktop application built with Tauri, Rust, and TypeScript, tailored for the Hermes-CN-Core runtime. It includes health checks, update management, and integration with Feishu.
entries.liftaris/herm: An alternative TUI and dashboard built with OpenTUI that renders markdown, LaTeX, and tool calls. It uses Kanban boards to monitor agentic work and provides terminal-based management of profiles and environment configs.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: A mobile-first React SPA designed as a Telegram Mini App with a terminal-style UI for agent management. It implements secure HMAC-SHA256 and Ed25519 authentication and supports vision analysis for file attachments.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows desktop GUI that runs without Docker or admin rights, integrating local models via LM Studio. It comes pre-loaded with over 100 tools, including ComfyUI and a dynamic tool maker for creating API wrappers.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: A glassmorphic web interface consisting of a single-file React 18 application and a lightweight Python proxy. It provides real-time log streaming and a live workspace file browser for self-hosted assistants.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A process monitoring and configuration dashboard that tracks costs and model distribution via a real-time activity feed. It allows for read-only inspection of skill sources and full-text search of conversation histories.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A self-hosted workspace that combines a chat-first interface with tool approval cards and SSE streaming. It includes an integrated marketplace for discovering MCP servers and isolated profile management for separate API keys and memory.
entries.Felix-Forever/hermes-agent-desktop: A multi-agent desktop client that orchestrates collaboration between 20 specialized AI agents via a PM orchestrator. It features a visual Skill Store with over 50 curated one-click installations and streaming SSE responses.
